Preflop 3-bet with Ah9h was strong, isolating against Minni's button raise and Flash's loose SB call. Minni's call of the 3-bet from button suggests a decent range: medium pairs (77-TT), Ax suited, suited connectors (45s-87s), broadway, or sets. Flop Jc6d5d is draw-heavy: diamond flush draws, gutshots (78, 34, 47), open-enders (78 for 4-9 straight). I have ace-high with no pair, no straight draw, no flush draw—pure showdown value and backdoor heart flush. OOP first to act in 2300 pot against shorter-stacked Minni (10k effective). Board hits villain's range moderately (J hits broadway, 65 suited connectors possible), so c-betting risks calls from better aces/pairs/draws and folds from worse (missed broadway). Checking retains initiative flexibility: induces bluffs from missed draws, gets value from worse (KQ, AJ) on turns, or free showdown. Folding not needed pre-turn; betting too thin given no equity multipliers. Check to pot control and realize equity.

I have top pair (J) with a good Q kicker on a relatively dry J♣ 6♦ 5♦ flop, and I’m in position after Elon (the 3-bettor) checked. Checking lets him see cheap cards that can outdraw me (4/7 for a straight or a diamond if he has two diamonds), and is too passive vs a wide 3-bet range. A bet extracts value from worse Jx, medium pairs and Ax/AQ type hands, and charges the few draw combos. I’ll size around half-pot to get called by worse and deny equity without bloating the pot out of position — 1,150 into a 2,300 pot. If Elon check-raises big it will usually indicate a set/overpair and I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to large, commit to small-to-medium sizing), but for now a half-pot value/protection bet is the right play.

Facing a half-pot bet on a draw-heavy Jc6d5d flop after checking my 3-bet range. Ah9h is ace-high with only backdoor heart potential and no immediate draws or pairs. Minni's button call of my 3-bet suggests a strong-ish range: medium pairs (77-JJ), AJs+, suited connectors (45s-78s), diamond flush draws. The board hits her range well with Jx, pairs, and draws. Calling 1150 into 3450 offers ~3:1 but my equity is <20% against her value/draw range OOP. Raising as a bluff risks her shoving better or calling with equity; pot control preflop check was to fold to strong bets like this. Effective stacks favor folding to preserve stack for better spots in tournament with 68 hands left.

I am on the button with 6h 5s, a very weak offsuit hand. While I am looking for opportunities to build my 37.5 BB stack, opening with 65o against a chip leader in the small blind and an active big blind is unnecessarily risky. I will wait for a better spot to commit chips.

Preflop in the small blind facing a fold from the button. My hand is J5o, a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and playability out of position against the big blind. Completing the small blind gives good pot odds (50 to call into 150, about 3:1), but postflop OOP, this hand struggles against a typical BB calling range—it will be dominated often, hard to realize equity, and vulnerable to raises. With deep stacks (23,600 effective vs BB's 12,500), folding preserves stack for better spots. History shows Minni is active but no need to speculate with trash. Standard fold to maximize EV long-term.

3-handed play on the button, acting first preflop with blinds posted (pot 150, to call 100). 92dd is a suited hand with flush potential and live cards, well within the wide opening range from this position in short-handed play. Minni (SB, 126BB deep) and Flash (BB, ~36BB) likely defend wide but our position postflop is excellent. Raising steals blinds frequently and allows continuation postflop if called. Standard 3x open to 300 pressures the shorter BB stack especially. Folding would be too tight.

Facing Elon's button open to 300 (pot 450, 250 to call). I have T♠4♠ — a suited ten is playable but marginal, and I'm out of position postflop. With 126BB I can realize equity, but I don't want to 3-bet OOP with such a weak hand (and there’s a short-stacked big blind who could jam). Calling is the best option: it gets a cheap flop, preserves stack, and keeps weaker parts of Elon's wide opening range in. Plan: fold to a sizeable preflop 3-bet (>~900) or to heavy postflop aggression unless I have a real piece of the board (pair of tens or better, a strong spade draw, or a good two-way hand). If I hit a strong draw or pair, play aggressively; if I miss and face C-bets, give up. I'll call the 250 required (total committed = 300).

I am in the big blind with Ace-Eight offsuit (Ac 8h). Elon (Button) raised to 300, and Minni (Small Blind) called. I am getting excellent pot odds of 3.5 to 1 (200 to call into a pot of 700) to see a flop. Given the strength of an Ace-high hand in a three-way pot and the favorable odds, a call is the most standard and efficient play here. I will defend my big blind and evaluate the strength of my hand on the flop while being mindful of my stack size.

Flop is 5♠-5♣-2♥ — a dry, paired board. I hold T♠4♠ and have no pair, only a weak backdoor spade possibility (need two more spades) and very little showdown value. This is a 3‑way pot and I'm out of position, so bluffing/CB is ineffective and I should preserve my stack for better spots. I'll check and let the preflop raiser (Elon) act first. Plan: fold to a significant c‑bet or any raise; consider calling a very small probe (≈≤1/3 pot) to realize equity and see a turn that actually improves me (a ten, four, or spade). If Elon checks back, take a free turn to try to pick up backdoor equity or improve before committing chips.

On a very dry board of 5-5-2 rainbow, my Ace-high has significant showdown value and could currently be the best hand. Minni has checked, and since Elon was the pre-flop aggressor, the standard play is to check to the raiser to see how he reacts. Betting here doesn't achieve much as I likely only get called by better hands or folds from hands I'm already beating. I will check and re-evaluate based on Elon's action.

Flop board is 5s-5c-2h, giving me two pair (fives and twos) with 9d-2d, a very strong hand likely best against the field. Paired board reduces straight/flush draws, but possible gutshots or weaker pairs. Both opponents checked, suggesting weakness or traps, but as preflop raiser in position with initiative, a value bet extracts from worse hands like single pair, ace-high, or draws. Pot is 900; Flash is short-stacked at 3450 (~3.5x pot), so a pot-sized bet of 900 builds the pot maximally while putting max pressure on him and charging Minni. Avoids check-raise risks and protects against overcards or runner-runner draws. Optimal for stack maximization in 3-handed play.

Preflop I called with T♠4♠ to see a cheap flop. The flop 5♠-5♣-2♥ is very dry and paired — I have no pair and only a runner‑runner spade flush (very unlikely). Elon’s bet 900 into a 1800 pot (~1/2 pot) is larger than the small probe size I was willing to call; calling requires ~33% equity (900/(1800+900)) which T4s does not have against a realistic c‑bet range (pocket pairs, 5x, and many overcards). There’s also a short stacked big blind (Flash, 3450) who could jam and put me in a tough spot. Raising OOP as a bluff is unnecessary and risky. I’ll fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

On a very dry, paired board of 5-5-2, Elon has fired a pot-sized bet of 900 into 900 after both Minni and I checked. While my Ace-high has some showdown value and could technically be the best hand if Elon is C-betting with air or lower overcards, a full pot-sized bet into two players on this specific board texture is quite polarizing. It often represents either a monster (like a 5 or a high pocket pair) or a determined bluff. Given that I have 3450 chips remaining, calling 900 represents more than 25% of my stack. Without a clear draw and being out of position for the rest of the hand against a massive stack, I cannot justify the risk of calling. I will preserve my stack for a more favorable opportunity.

I'm on the button with Q6s — a suited queen with decent playability in position in a 3-handed pot. I want to take the initiative and steal the blinds or isolate heads-up, so a standard open to 300 (≈3x the BB) gives good fold equity while keeping the pot manageable. If Flash (short stack) shoves or Elon 3-bets to a large size I'll fold — Q6s is too weak to commit a lot out of position — but if called I'll use position to c-bet favorable flops and play postflop for value when I connect or for pot control otherwise.

I am holding 92o, which is one of the weakest hands in poker. Minni has raised from the button, and I am in the small blind. Defending with such a weak hand out of position is a losing play in the long run. Given my stack size is the smallest at the table, I need to be more selective with the hands I choose to play. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Pocket 66 is a strong hand against a button raise, which is often wide due to position. Minni shows aggression throughout history, raising frequently and betting postflop often. 3-betting isolates, takes initiative OOP, and leverages equity dominance over her range (lower pairs crushed, ahead vs suited broadways/connectors). Pot odds excellent anyway, but building pot with premium maximizes EV. Size to 900 (3x her 300), standard preflop. Deep stacks (24k effective vs her 12k) support this; sets well OOP if called. Calling too passive, gives her position for free.

Call to 900. Q6s is marginal but playable in position — Elon’s 3-bet to 900 gives me decent pot odds (600 to call into a 1250 pot ≈ 32% equity needed) and the button lets me see the flop last and control postflop. A 4-bet bluff with Q6s is too thin against a deep, capable opponent who can 5-bet or shove; folding is too passive after investing 300 and losing positional advantage. Plan: call, see a flop, proceed aggressively when I hit (top pair, strong draws) and exercise pot control / fold to heavy action on dry boards; fold to large 4-bets/5-bet shoves unless I have a clear read Elon is spewy.

Preflop 3-bet with 66 as BB vs button call was strong, isolating and taking initiative OOP. Flop A♦8♠7♣ is somewhat coordinated (straight draws like 56, 9T) but rainbow, missing many flush draws. 66 is a strong underpair OOP: beats her lighter Ax (A9-A2, weak kickers), all lower pairs, bluffs/floaters, suited connectors without improvement. Vulnerable to sets (77/88/AA/AK/AQ hit hard) but ahead of much of her 3bet-call range (wide button: AJs+, KQs, 98s, TT+ crushed pre but postflop equity ok). CBet for value/protection, as she calls stations often per history (e.g., Hand 26,43,67) but folds to aggression sometimes (Hand1,51). OOP demands thin value; checking induces bluffs but gives free cards to OESD/draws. Size 2/3 pot (1200) balances value vs floats, builds pot OOP with equity advantage. Her 11k stack allows multi-street play; exploits her aggression.

Elon 3‑bet preflop and now fires 1,200 into a 3,050 pot on A♦‑8♠‑7♣. I have Q♠6♠ — one overcard and only a runner‑runner spade possibility, so my realistic outs are very slim (basically three queens and some weak pair/runner scenarios). Calling 1,200 into an effective pot of 4,250 requires ~28% equity; versus a 3‑bet/c‑bet range heavy in A‑x, AK, big pairs and strong broadways I’m well below that. Raising as a bluff is too thin here and would risk too much with minimal fold equity. I’ll let this one go and preserve chips for a better spot in position.

I am on the button with a very weak hand (6c 2d). My stack is currently 3,400, which is relatively short compared to my opponents. Playing weak hands out of position or in multi-way pots will only bleed chips. The best course of action is to fold and wait for a better spot to double up or build my stack.

Jd9s is a strong suited connector with good playability heads-up versus the BB after the button folds. From the SB, raising takes initiative, builds the pot, and exploits a likely wide defending range from Minni based on her history of calling raises and limping. Standard open-raise size of 3x the BB (300 total) balances aggression and pot odds. Deep stacks allow for post-flop flexibility.

Elon opened to 300 from the SB; I’m in the BB with Td3d. Pot is 400 and I need 200 to call. Heads‑up SB opens are wide and my hand has some playability (suited Ten with backdoor diamond/straight potential). Stacks are deep enough to realize equity postflop (I have 11,350 vs his ~24,850), and I’m out of position vs the opener, so 3‑betting as a bluff is unnecessary and high variance. I’ll just call to see a flop — proceed aggressively if I hit a ten/diamond or strong draw, but fold to heavy aggression with a complete miss.

Preflop, raised Jd9s from SB as a strong suited connector to take initiative OOP vs BB defender. Flop Ac8sKc hits a broadway-heavy board with two clubs. I have a gutshot straight draw to the T (for 8-9-T-J-K), backdoor spade flush draw, and positionally as the preflop raisor, my range is perceived to include Ax, Kx, broadways which block her value. Minni's BB calling range is wide based on history (frequent calls/limps). Pot 600, OOP but deep stacks allow semi-bluff c-bet to fold out her worse hands like weak Ax, dominated broadways, or draws. Small sizing 300 (half pot) maximizes fold equity while keeping pot small if called, preserving fold equity for turn. Checking risks giving free cards and losing initiative.

Board is A♣ 8♠ K♣ and I have T♦3♦ — no pair and no immediate diamond on board, so my raw equity is low versus a c-bet range that contains Ax/Kx. Elon opens a wide SB range and c-bets frequently here; his bet is small (300 into a 900 pot) so I’m being offered ~25% pot odds. Calling is a cheap probe to realize equity and keep his bluffs/missed broadway hands in the pot. I won’t inflate the pot out of position with a bluff or 3‑bet; plan is to fold to significant turn aggression unless I hit (pair a Ten or 3, or pick up a strong draw), or to continue only vs a check. Therefore I will call the 300 to see the turn.

Turn board brings the 4s, pairing nothing but adding another spade, improving my hand to a strong spade flush draw (9 outs) plus live gutshot straight draw to the T (4 outs, some overlap), giving me around 12-15% equity heads-up even against top pair/top kicker. Pot is 1200, OOP after Minni called flop c-bet on scary AcKc8s board. Her range: Ax (likely clubs or weak), Kx, sets (AA/KK unlikely given action), flush draws, two-pair. Checking risks giving a free card to her draws/completed hands or facing a bet where I have tough decisions. Semi-bluff betting maintains aggression from pre/cbet, charges her draws/Ax, folds out worse like weak A/K high, and builds pot for river shove if we hit. Sizing 800 (2/3 pot) offers good fold equity vs her capped range while keeping SPR manageable (~10:1 post-bet). Minni's history shows she calls but folds to continued pressure often (e.g., Hands 5,9,14); deep stacks favor realizing equity when called.

Board: A♣ 8♠ K♣ 4♠; I hold T♦3♦. Elon (SB) opened wide pre and c‑bet the flop; I called as a probe. On the turn he now bets 800 into a 2000 pot. Calling 800 to win 2800 requires ~28.6% equity. With no pair and no real draw I only have 6 outs to pair by the river (~13% to hit), well below the break‑even equity. Raising OOP as a bluff here is thin (high variance, little fold equity vs his range/stack). I also explicitly planned to fold to significant turn aggression unless I’d paired or picked up a strong draw. Therefore folding is the +EV choice to preserve my stack for a better spot.

Preflop on the button with Qh6d, a speculative queen-high hand with position. Blinds posted (pot 150, to call 100), likely folds to me. Raising from the button has high fold equity against blinds, especially with Flash short-stacked at 33BB (may fold or shove wide, but Q6o has decent equity vs shoving range) and Minni deep. Standard button steal size: 3x BB to 300. Limping weak OOP postflop; folding too tight deep-stacked. Builds pot OOP if called or steals blinds frequently.

Facing a button open to 300 from the small blind with 6♠4♠. This is a very weak, low-suited hand with poor playability OOP. Calling 250 into a 450 pot gives me ~35.7% required equity (250/(450+250)), which is optimistic vs a BTN open range. A light 3-bet to 500 would be a bluff with little fold equity and risks getting called or facing a shove from the short-stacked BB (3,300) or a strong response from the button. With 10,800 behind and many better spots to pick up chips, the correct, lowest-variance play is to fold and preserve my stack for a better opportunity.

I am the short stack with 3,300 chips (33 BB). Elon has a massive lead and is opening from the button, likely with a very wide range. While I'm getting decent odds to call from the Big Blind (200 to win 450), K5o is a weak hand to play out of position against a big stack who can apply maximum pressure on future streets. I have enough big blinds to wait for a better spot or a hand with better post-flop playability to attempt a double-up.

On the button with 9♠5♣ — a weak, unsuited hand. Pot is 150 and the cost to see a flop is only 100 (1BB) which is a cheap price to realize position advantage. Raising to 200 risks inducing a shove/call from the shortish SB (Flash, ~32BB) or a wide defense from the deep BB (Elon), making the spot unnecessarily bloated with a poor hand. I’ll take the cheaper line: call 100, see the flop in position, and proceed based on the texture (c‑bet small when favorable, fold to significant aggression).

I am holding Ace-Ten offsuit in the small blind, which is a very strong hand for 3-handed play. The button has limped in, which often indicates a capped or weak range. With a relatively short stack of 32.5 BB, I need to be proactive. Raising here allows me to isolate the button limper, take control of the hand, and potentially win the pot immediately. I'll raise to 450 to put pressure on both the button and the big blind.

3-handed BB vs button limp and SB raise to 4.5BB from short stack (28.5BB effective). SB raise after limp is often wide for value/protection/steal, including Ax, suited connectors, broadway, small pairs. 86s is a speculative suited two-gapper with straight/flush potential and good playability multiway or heads-up. Pot odds excellent: calling 350 more into 650 pot (~1.86:1 immediate, plus implied from short stack). Position postflop on SB, Minni likely to fold or call light. Folding too tight in BB defense spot; raising risks multiway pot OOP with marginal hand. Flat to realize equity and control pot.

I originally planned to just limp and see a cheap flop, but Flash (SB) raised to 450 and Elon called, so the cost to continue is 350 more (to 450 total). The pot is ~1000, so calling 350 offers ~3.86:1 pot odds (≈20.5% equity required). 9♠5♣ offsuit has very poor multiway equity and is easily dominated; calling here in a three‑way pot is negative EV. A min 3‑bet to 800 would bloat the pot and risk a shove/call from the short SB or a strong defense from the deep BB — not profitable with this hand. I’ll fold and wait for a better, cleaner spot with position and initiative.

I have flopped top two pair (Aces and Tens) on a board of A-T-3 with two spades. This is an exceptionally strong hand. Since I was the pre-flop aggressor and Elon called from the big blind, I should continue with a value bet. Elon's range could include weaker Aces, Tens, or spade draws that will likely call a bet. I will bet 500, which is half the pot, to build value and protect my hand from potential draws while remaining small enough to keep him in with second-best hands.

Heads-up on the flop against short-stacked Flash (23.5BB effective) who raised preflop wide after button limp and now c-bets 1/3 pot on A-T-3 rainbow with two spades. My 8s6h gives me a flush draw (9 outs to the nuts, as board has As/3s), backdoor straight potential, and decent equity (~35-40% vs his polarized c-bet range including Ax, broadway, draws, bluffs). Folding a strong draw OOP vs short stack is too tight in this spot; raising risks bloating pot OOP when he can jam weaker Ax/draws and deny equity realization. Calling controls pot size, realizes implied odds from his short stack shoving turns we hit, and keeps fold equity on later streets. Positionally challenged but equity and dynamics favor continuing.

On this turn (6c), my top two pair (Aces and Tens) remains extremely strong. The board is relatively static, although a few backdoor draws have appeared. My stack-to-pot ratio is now around 1.17 (2350 remaining into a 2000 pot). I need to continue betting for value against Elon's range, which likely consists of weaker Aces, Tens, or flush draws. I'll bet 1000, which is half the pot, to build the pot further and set up a manageable all-in on the river, while still giving him a price to call with his drawing hands or marginal made hands.

Paired our 6h with the 6c turn card for bottom pair to go with our strong spade flush draw (8s, 9 outs to a strong flush), giving us substantial equity (~45%+ vs. Flash's likely Ax-heavy value range or polarized bluffs/draws from wide preflop raising spot after limp). His repeated half-pot bets flop and turn as short stack (13.5BB left) polarize to top pair+, sets, or air/draws trying to steal dead money. Calling realizes equity OOP profitably with excellent pot odds (1000 into 3000 ~3:1 immediate, superior vs bluffs), denies him free cards, and sets up river value if we improve or check-raise his likely value bet/shove. Raising to ~2000-2350 folds out bluffs we dominate and gets called by better Ax; better to keep weaker hands in and leverage deep stack advantage post-river. Consistent with prior calls exploiting wide range and short-stack dynamics.

I have top two pair (Aces and Tens) on a board of Tc-3s-As-6c-2s. While the river 2s completes a possible spade flush and a straight (4-5), these are relatively small parts of Elon's range given his calls on the flop and turn. My stack-to-pot ratio is extremely low (1350 remaining into a 4000 pot), meaning I am pot-committed with a hand this strong. Checking might allow Elon to check behind with weaker hands that would have called a small shove, or induce a bluff, but the most straightforward play to extract final value from weaker Aces or pairs is to move all-in. I will bet my remaining 1350 chips.

River bet of 1350 (all-in) into 5350 pot offers excellent odds: 1350 to win 6700 (~4:1, needing ~19% equity). Heads-up vs short-stacked Flash's polarized triple-barrel range from wide SB preflop raise after limp (Ax, sets, combo draws, two-pair, missed draws/bluffs like broadway/KQ/KJs, suited connectors). Board A♥T♣6♣3♠2♠ completes spades flushes (any 2 spades, esp suited Ax) and wheel straights (A5432 with 54s/54o). We hold bottom pair 6s (beats bluffs, missed draws, weaker Ax) with 8s blocker to lower flushes/medium flush draws. No showdown value vs nuts (AA/sets/Ax top, flushes, wheel) but ~25-35% equity vs polarized range (wins vs ~30% bluffs/missed draws common from his spot/line, live outs minimal but pair live vs weak Ax). Consistent with prior calls exploiting his wide range, short-stack dynamics, and pot control OOP; folding bottom pair OOP after calling down draw is -EV vs aggressive shorty. Call realizes equity profitably, consistent with solver-approved defense.
I am on the button with Jack-Ten offsuit in a 3-handed game. This is a standard opening hand. I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and leverage my position against the blinds.

Preflop, facing a button raise to 300 (3BB) from Flash with A9o in the SB. A9o has good equity against a wide button opening range, which likely includes many weaker aces, broadways, suited connectors, etc. Calling OOP is suboptimal due to position disadvantage and vulnerability to domination by stronger aces. Folding is too tight given the pot odds (calling 250 into 450) and implied odds with deep stacks. Best play is to 3-bet for value/isolation, sizing to 900 (3x the raise). This pressures Flash's wide range, builds the pot with our strong ace-high hand, and denies equity to junk. Minni in BB can call or squeeze, but our hand plays well postflop. Stack sizes support this: my 22.5k deep, Flash 6.4k effective.

I'm on the big blind with K♦J♦ facing a button open to 300 and a SB 3‑bet to 900. The call is 800 more into a 1300 pot — a small investment (~7.5% of my 10,600 stack) for a playable, suited broadway that has good equity and postflop playability. Folding is too tight given the price; a 4‑bet (even to the min 1500) won't reliably fold Elon and would bloat a pot out of position against two players. I'll call to see a flop, keep the pot manageable, and proceed cautiously postflop — folding to heavy aggression unless I hit or have strong draws, and extracting value when I make top pair or better.

I am on the button with Jack-Ten offsuit. I initially raised to 300, but faced a 3-bet to 900 from Elon in the Small Blind and a cold call from Minni in the Big Blind. The pot is now 2100 and I need to call 600 more. This gives me pot odds of 3.5:1 (600 to win 2100). Given the multi-way nature and the fact that I have position on both players post-flop, Jack-Ten has enough playability to justify a call, even if I'm likely behind Elon's 3-betting range. I will call to see the flop.

Preflop, I 3-bet A9o as a strong ace-high hand against Flash's button open, isolating and building the pot. Now on flop 4h-9d-2s rainbow, I have top pair top kicker (pair of 9s with Ad). Dry board with minimal draws (gutshots like 3x, 5T unlikely), favors my strong hand multiway. Pot 2700, first to act OOP. Betting for value against worse pairs (e.g., smaller pairs, weaker aces), overpairs that may have check-folded, and draws. Size to 1800 (~2/3 pot) denies equity, gets called by 1-2 pairs and sets, thin field if one folds. Flash effective 5800 behind, Minni 9800; stacks allow pressure. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or letting them realize equity cheaply. Goal: extract value and grow stack in tournament.

Elon (the SB 3-bettor) fires 1,800 into a 4,500 pot — calling requires ~28.6% equity (1,800 / (4,500+1,800)). I have K♦J♦: no pair on 4♥9♦2♠ but two overcards and a two-card diamond backdoor (needs both turn+river diamonds; low probability). Given I already invested preflop and my hand has reasonable showdown equity versus a 3‑bettor’s c-bet range (broadways, some pairs, some bluffs), calling to see the turn is the best line. Raising OOP here into two opponents will bloat the pot with little fold equity and is too thin without a pair or a strong draw; folding would give up too much equity after calling preflop. Plan: call 1,800 to keep the pot manageable, re-evaluate on the turn — fold to heavy further aggression unless I pair up or pick up a meaningful draw.

Pre-flop, I called a 3-bet in position with Jh Th. The flop is 4h 9d 2s, providing me with no pair and very little equity. Elon, the pre-flop aggressor, has bet 1800 (two-thirds of the pot) and Minni has called that bet. In a 3-way pot on such a dry board, Elon likely has a strong overpair or a high-equity hand, and Minni's call suggests she has at least a pair or a strong draw. With only 5800 chips remaining and Jack-high, calling 1800 is not justifiable as I am likely drawing to only a few outs if any. Folding is the only sensible play here.

Heads-up on turn vs Minni after Flash folded to my flop bet. I have top pair top kicker (9c Ad) on a board of 4h-9d-2s-6d. Pot 6300, first to act OOP with 19.9k stack, Minni 8k. The turn 6d brings diamond flush draws into play (two diamonds on board) and possible straight draws (e.g., 5-7, 3-5 wheel), making the board more draw-heavy. My hand has strong showdown value but is vulnerable to sets (99 unlikely as I have 9c), two-pair combos (e.g., 46, 96 preflop unlikely), or if Minni has Adx or better kicker. Preflop, Minni called my 3-bet squeeze OOP with a wide BB defending range, then called flop bet into multiway pot, so her range includes pairs (77-88, JJ+), weaker Ax/9x, diamond draws (Adx, KdQd etc.), floats. Betting now risks: 1) Overvaluing vs better hands/draws; 2) Getting check-raised; 3) Pot control issues OOP deepstacked. Checking induces bluffs/missed draws from her floaty range, allows pot control, realizes equity cheaply if she checks behind, and sets up river check-raise if she overbets bluffs (her history shows aggressive rivers). Thin value bet (e.g., 3k) gets called by worse but folds better drawing hands; however, with improved draws and OOP, check is balanced, exploits her calling station tendencies by trapping. Long-term tournament goal favors controlled aggression but pot control here preserves stack while extracting max value over 150 hands.

I hold K♦J♦ on 4♥‑9♦‑2♠‑6♦: no pair but I picked up a strong diamond draw (K♦J♦ + 9♦+6♦ = 4 diamonds on board → 9 diamond outs) and I also have two overcards (3 K/J outs each). That’s 9 outs to a flush (≈19.6% on the river) and 6 additional non‑diamond K/J outs (≈13.0%), ~15/46 ≈ 32.6% to improve to at least a pair/flush by showdown (noting some river cards can still lose to better hands). Elon checked the turn after barreling the flop, which often signals range weakness or pot control — a good spot to apply pressure. Betting gives fold equity against his checked range and builds the pot when I do hit. The pot is 6,300; a bet of 3,000 (~47% pot) requires ~32.3% fold frequency to break even as a pure bluff, which is realistic here. Plan: semi‑bluff the turn with a 3,000 bet — if called I still have strong equity to the river; if Elon check‑raises large (commits a big portion of my stack) I’ll fold this draw, but I’ll call smaller raises that give correct pot odds.

Heads-up on turn vs Minni with TPTK (Ad9c) on 4h-9d-2s-6d board, pot 6300 pre-turn action. I checked as planned to induce bluffs from her floaty BB calling range (pairs below 99, weaker Ax/9x, diamond draws now live with 6d), setting up a check-raise. She bets 3000 (~half pot), representing value (sets/two-pair unlikely given preflop action and flop call OOP multiway), thin value (weaker pairs/draws), or bluffs (missed floats). Pot odds excellent (9300 pot, call 3000 into 12300 = 4:1), but raising accomplishes multiple goals: extracts value from worse (e.g., 88-77, A-high diamond draws), denies equity to live draws (e.g., AdKd, flush draws), protects vs her aggression, and leverages short stacks (Minni ~5k behind post-bet, me 19.9k). Min raise to 6000 (2x her bet) sizes well - gets max value if called by worse, folds pure bluffs/draws; larger risks commitment OOP but tournament stage (89/150, deep effective) favors building pot when ahead vs calling station tendencies in history. Folding too tight for strength/SDV; flat risks free river for draws/overbets. Raise balances check-raise range, exploits her history of calling big (e.g., Hand 26,43,67).

Elon check‑raised my turn bet to 6,000; I face 3,000 to call into a 15,300 pot and would have 2,000 left if I call. Required equity to break even on the call = 3,000 / (15,300+3,000) ≈ 16.4%. My hand (K♦J♦) on 4♥‑9♦‑2♠‑6♦: I have four diamonds (two in hand + two on board) — 9 diamond outs to make a flush on the river (~19.6%), plus the three remaining kings and three remaining jacks (6 additional outs) to make a pair. Naively that’s ≈15 outs or ~32.6% to improve to at least a pair/flush by showdown (some K/J pair-outs can still be behind, but even a discounted equity comfortably beats the 16.4% threshold). Elon’s line (3‑bet pre, c‑bet flop, check on turn then check‑raise) includes strong value but also floats/draws and some bluffs — calling is +EV here. Shoving would risk too much fold equity loss and overcommit a large portion of my stack; folding surrenders too much equity. Plan: call the 3,000 to realize my strong draw/overcard equity, then re‑evaluate on the river — if I hit a pair/flush I’ll extract value, if I miss I’ll fold to significant aggression with only 2,000 left.

River 7d completes the diamond flush draw (three diamonds on board: 9d, 6d, 7d) and potentially opens low straight draws (e.g., 3-5, 5-8 combos unlikely but possible with 2-4-6-7). My Ad9c is top pair top kicker (TPTK) from flop, unimproved, with Ad blocking nut flush draws. Pot 18300, OOP first to act heads-up vs Minni (2000 behind, pot-committed effectively). Her range for flatting pre (BB vs button iso-3bet), calling flop c-bet multiway OOP (dry 492 rainbow), then betting turn 6d after my check (~half pot), facing my check-raise to 6000 OOP (calling final 3000 with ~5k left): polarized to strong made hands (sets 22/44/66/99 unlikely as board 9d and I hold 9c; two-pair 96/24/46 unlikely preflop), overpairs JJ+, weaker Ax (A8-AQ), or diamond flush draws (AdX blocked somewhat, KdQd+ suited gappers/connectors feasible BB defend). Many diamond draws now hit flush; unlikely she missed entirely given sizing aggression. Betting risks: thin value from worse Ax/pairs gets called only by flushes/straights better; high fold equity low as she's committed (2000 into 18300+bet >9:1 odds). Checking exploits: induces bluffs from missed value (overpairs wary of board, weaker pairs), pot control OOP vulnerable, huge implied if she donk-shoves bluffs/medium (call 2000 into ~20k pot ~10:1, my TPTK/SDV has ~30-40% vs polarized range with blockers); consistent with turn trap plan. Tournament mid-stage (89/150), preserve deep stack (13.9k) vs shorty, deny Flash future sweat. Check balances river range (value monsters check too), maximizes EV long-term.

I have K♦J♦ and the river 7♦ completes a king‑high diamond flush — the second‑nut (only A♦ beats me). Elon’s line (3‑bet pre, c‑bet flop, check on the turn then a check‑raise that I called) is polarized but contains many hands that will call a small all‑in on the river: single pairs, two‑pair/sets that slowed down, and diamond combos worse than Kx♦. The pot is 18,300 and my effective chips are 2,000 — shoving my remaining stack prices Elon to call for ~9.9% equity (2,000/(18,300+2,000)), so he will call with a wide set of worse hands and I can extract value. Fold equity isn’t required; this is a clean value jam with the second nut. I’ll bet my remaining 2,000 to get called by worse and maximize my stack.

River 7d completes the diamond flush (9d-6d-7d) that was heavily live after turn 6d, transforming many semi-bluffs/value calls from Minni's BB defending range into monsters. My Ad9c TPTK blocks Ad nut flush but vulnerable to Kd/Qd/Jd/Td/8d+ flushes. Minni's line is nutted: calls pre OOP multiway vs iso-3bet (diamond suited connectors/gappers like KdQd, JdTd feasible), calls flop 1800 cbet into 3-way pot on dry board (Ax/9x/draws), donk bets turn 3000 after my check (~1/2 pot aggression reps value/draw), hero-calls my 6000 check-raise (committing ~80% stack, folds bluffs/draws that miss), then after my river check jams last 2000 into 18300 pot. Bluffing range crushed: can't credibly rep flush/overpair here after calling off deep. Value range: flushes, unlikely sets (22/44/66 rare given action/board), 97 two-pair possible but thin. Pot odds ~11:1 (2000 into ~22300) require ~8% equity, but vs polarized nuts/bluffs my equity <20% (dead vs flush ~10% vs two-pair/SDV, 0% vs flush). Tournament mid (89/150), deep 139BB stack; folding preserves vs shorty Minni/Flash, avoids spew OOP. Check trap failed to induce fold; live to fight 61 more hands maximizing stack. Fold.

AK suited is one of the best hands in poker, especially on the button where I have position on both opponents. The pot is small at 150 with only 100 to call, likely a limp from SB and check or post from BB. Raising builds the pot for value, takes initiative, and leverages my position. Minni is deep-stacked and aggressive from history, Flash has 5700 (shorter stack). A standard raise size around 4x the BB or to 400 total pressures them while keeping good pot odds. Folding or calling is suboptimal with this hand.

I have K♠3♣ in the small blind facing a button open to 400 (pot 550, 350 to call). K3o is a very weak, frequently dominated hand vs a BTN opening range (lots of Kx/Ax/broadways). Calling 350 puts me out of position and likely into a multiway pot if the big blind defends; my equity isn’t sufficient to justify the price (350 into a 900 pot ≈ 39% break-even) and I don’t want to play a bloated OOP pot with a raggy hand. 3-betting as a bluff is unattractive from the SB here — I’ll be OOP, a min 3-bet won’t fold many better hands, and there’s a short-stacked BB who could complicate the spot. Preserve stack for better opportunities and fold.

In this hand, I am in the Big Blind with 6d 7c. Elon, on the button, has raised to 400. Minni in the Small Blind has folded. My hand, 6-7 off-suit, is relatively weak and does not play well out of position against a button open, especially a 4x raise. While the pot odds are roughly 2.8:1, the low equity of this hand and the disadvantage of being first to act post-flop make this a clear fold. I prefer to preserve my stack of 57 BBs for better spots as the shortest stack at the table.

I’m on the button with 9♣7♥ — a marginal but playable hand in position against only the blinds. With pot 150 and only 100 to call, I’ll open to a standard 3xBB (300) to capitalize on my positional advantage and exert fold equity on the small blind and big blind. The sizing is small relative to the stacks (keeps the pot manageable if I miss) but large enough to force some weak defenses. Plan post‑flop: use position to c‑bet on dry boards and proceed cautiously on coordinated/ace‑high textures. If I face a sizable 3‑bet (large relative to my raise) I will fold this hand; I’ll continue only against small squeezes or calls where pot odds and implied odds make sense.

I am holding Ace-Jack offsuit (As Jh) in the small blind. In a three-handed game, this is a very strong hand. Minni, who is currently the chip leader, has opened from the button. I want to 3-bet here to gain the initiative and potentially end the hand immediately or narrow the field before the flop. A raise to 1000 (roughly 3.3x the opening raise) is a standard sizing from the small blind that puts pressure on the button and the big blind.

Ten-Five offsuit is a speculative trash hand with poor playability, dominated equity, and reverse implied odds out of position. Facing a button raise to 3BB and small blind 3-bet to 10BB, Flash's range is strong (pairs, broadways, suited aces), and Minni can 4-bet or call wide but still ahead. Pot odds to call 900 into 1400 (~1.5:1) insufficient for <20% equity needed. No fold equity on a call or raise viable with this holding. Folding preserves stack for better spots in tournament.

Flash 3-bet to 1000 after my open to 300. The pot is ~1400 and I must call 700 to continue. 9♣7♥ is a marginal, unsuited hand with poor equity vs a standard 3‑betting range; calling 700 requires ~33% equity to break even, and 9‑7o will usually have substantially less vs a polarized or even moderately wide 3‑bet range. 4‑betting (min to 1700) or shoving as a bluff is high variance and unnecessary with a ~200BB stack. With position and many hands left in the tournament, preserving my stack and folding marginal spots is the +EV play here. I’ll fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with a pocket pair of threes. While 33 is a small pair, it's a strong enough hand to open for a raise in a three-handed game to try and take down the blinds or set up a post-flop play. I have 61 big blinds, which gives me enough room to open and evaluate if I face a three-bet. A standard 3x raise to 300 has been my consistent sizing throughout the tournament.

Pocket Kings is one of the strongest preflop hands, especially facing a button raise from Flash, who likely has a wide range. Standard play is to 3-bet for value and to isolate, as KK dominates most of Flash's continuing range. Minni in BB may call or 4-bet, but KK is still ahead. Sizing to 900 (3x the open) builds the pot nicely without overcommitting given stacks (Flash 5800 effective). Avoids flat calling to prevent multiway pots where AA or sets could be trouble.

Facing a 3-bet to 900 by Elon after Flash opened to 300, the pot is ~1300 and I must call 800 (need ≈38% equity). My hand 9♣8♠ (off-suit connector) has poor equity vs a typical SB 3-bet range (value-heavy: TT+/AQ+ and strong broadways) and playing a 3-way pot out of position is unattractive. A small 4-bet to 1500 would be a thin bluff unlikely to fold out better hands and would commit chips unnecessarily. With deep stacks and plenty of hands left, folding preserves my stack and avoids a low-expected-value situation. Fold.

I am on the button with pocket threes and have already put in 300. Elon has three-bet to 900 from the small blind. In a three-handed game, pocket pairs hold significant value, although 33 is the smallest. Calling the additional 600 represents about 10% of my remaining stack. While the 'Rule of 20' for set-mining suggests I should have more depth, the fact that we are three-handed means ranges are wider, and I will have the benefit of position for the rest of the hand. Elon has shown a high frequency of aggression, and folding here might be too tight given the pot odds and position.

Pocket Kings is an overpair on a 9d8d5s flop, which is relatively dry but features a diamond flush draw and some straight draws (e.g., 76, T7). Flash called my preflop 3-bet from the button, so his range is polarized: strong hands like QQ-JJ, AK, AQ, or bluffs/draws like suited connectors (T9s, 76s), Ax suited diamonds. KK crushes his value range and is live against sets/straights unlikely in his range. With pot at 1900 and effective stacks ~5200, betting for value and protection denies equity to draws. Sizing to ~2/3 pot (1200) builds the pot while keeping pressure on floats/draws without overbetting. Checking risks giving free cards to improve hands that beat us.

On this flop of 9d-8d-5s, my pocket threes are an underpair and highly vulnerable. Elon 3-bet pre-flop from the small blind and has now followed up with a substantial continuation bet of 1200 into a 1900 pot. This board texture hits a 3-betting range quite well (overpairs, sets like 99/88, or big draws like AdKd). Calling 1200 represents a large portion of my remaining stack, and I would likely face another bet on the turn. Since I am almost certainly behind and have very little equity to improve, folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack.

3-handed play on the button in an unopened pot (SB limp/check, BB check) with 6s9d offsuit. This hand has decent playability, straight potential, and position advantage. Button ranges are very wide 3-handed (~50-70%+), and 69o ranks well within that. Raising builds the pot, takes initiative, and can fold out worse hands. Pot is 150, calling 100 gets multiway odds but denies equity realization. Raise to 300 (3x) is standard sizing: not minraise to avoid weak domination, balances vs Flash's 51BB stack which may defend/shove wide, Minni deep but limped weak range. EV+ to isolate/steal blinds money.

Holding K♥6♣ in the small blind vs a button open to 300 is marginal but calling is cheap. The extra 250 is a very small fraction of my 19.8k stack and lets me realize equity against a wide button opening range. I don't want to 3-bet/raise with this weak offsuit hand because Flash (BB, 5.1k) can reraise and put me in a tough spot out of position; a small squeeze isn't worth the risk. Plan: call to see the flop, fold to any sizable preflop 3-bet, and postflop proceed only with meaningful top-pair/two-pair or strong draws — otherwise check-fold to aggression.

I have AKo in the Big Blind, which is a premium hand. Elon raised from the button, and Minni called from the small blind. This is a prime opportunity for a squeeze play. Raising will allow me to take control of the hand, potentially win the pot pre-flop, or build a pot where I likely have the best hand. I will raise to 1200.

Facing a 3-bet from Flash in the BB after my button open and Minni's call from SB. 6s9d is a speculative offsuit hand with decent playability multiway but poor equity against a BB 3-bet range (likely QQ+, AK, some bluffs but polarized). Pot is 1800, calling 900 commits ~10% of my deep stack into a pot that will be multiway (Minni likely continues with a wide calling range). Equity realization suffers OOP postflop if called/shoved. Raising adds fold equity but 69o is not strong enough for 4-bet jamming or isolating vs Flash's 40BB shove range. Folding preserves my deep stack (146BB) for better spots in 3-handed play; standard discipline vs aggression. Long-term EV+ to fold and re-steal later.

Flash (BB) 3-bet to 1,200 after a 300 open. To call I must put in 900 more to win a 2,700 pot, so I need ~33% equity. K♥6♣ offsuit has well under that versus a typical BB 3‑bet range (pockets, AK/AQ, strong broadways) and I’ll be out of position. Raising is reckless — Flash (4,000) can shove and force a terrible decision. Calling is cheap but mathematically unlikely +EV here and goes against my preflop plan to fold to a sizable 3‑bet. I’ll preserve my 19,550 stack for better spots.

I’m on the button with 5♦3♥ and only the blinds are in. This is a weak, non‑connecting offsuit hand, so I don’t want to limp and play a tricky multiway pot out of position. I have excellent positional advantage and large fold equity versus the SB (Flash, 5750) and BB (Elon, 14550). Raise size: I’ll open to 300 (a standard ~3x BB here, above the 200 minimum) to pressure the blinds and either take down the pot preflop or play postflop in position with initiative. If I get significant resistance (a big 3‑bet) I’ll fold 5‑3o; if called I’ll c‑bet favorable flops and release to heavy action without improvement.

I am in the Small Blind with Kh 7s. Minni, who has been very active from the button, has raised to 300. I need to call 250 more to see a flop in a pot that will be at least 700. In a three-handed game, King-high is relatively strong against a wide button opening range. Although I will be out of position, the pot odds (2.8:1) are favorable enough to justify a call and see a flop.

3-handed with deep stacks. Button (Minni) raises wide from BTN, SB (Flash) defends wide by calling. A6o is a solid defensive hand in BB: Ace blocker, decent high card value, playability for top pair or draws. Excellent pot odds (200 into 700, ~22% equity needed vs wide ranges). Prefer calling over folding to realize equity postflop; 3-bet too thin without position initiative. Will play cautiously postflop if multiway.

On a flop of 8s-9d-6d, I have an open-ended straight draw with my 7s. This is a very connected board that likely hits the ranges of both the pre-flop raiser (Minni) and the big blind caller (Elon). As the first player to act, I will check to see how the action develops. This allows me to see if I can get a cheap card to improve my hand or evaluate the size of a bet from Minni, who has the positional advantage.

Flop action facing a checked SB in 3-way pot (pot 900). Hold bottom pair (6s) with strong Ace kicker on draw-heavy board: diamond flush draw, straight draws (T7, 75o/suited). BTN raiser (Minni) has wide range including Ax, overs, draws; SB defender (Flash) wide including worse pairs, draws. Equity realization good multiway but must protect/charge draws OOP. Donk bet for thin value, pot control, deny equity to villains' floats/draws. Size 2/3 pot (600) balances value from worse (A-high, weaker pairs) and protection. Effective SPR low with Flash's 5500 stack. If check, Minni likely bets draws/value and we face tough spot OOP.

Fold. I opened preflop as a steal, but the flop 8♠-9♦-6♦ completely misses 5♦3♥ — no pair, no flush draw, and no realistic straight draw (only remote runner-runner possibilities). Elon’s bet of 600 into a 1500 pot gives ~29% pot odds to call, but my hand has essentially 0% equity versus a defending range of pairs, 8/9/6 combos, and diamond draws. A raise as a pure bluff is too thin here — it’s likely to be called by hands that beat me or to be met with a shove/re‑raise, and I don’t want to bloat a pot with air when two opponents can continue. Preserve the 19,250 stack for a better spot — fold.

I have an open-ended straight draw with Kh 7s on a board of 8s 9d 6d. Elon's bet of 600 into a 900 pot (making it 1500) gives me pot odds of approximately 28.5% (600/2100). With 8 outs to a straight, my chance of hitting on the turn is about 17%. While the immediate pot odds are slightly less than the direct odds of hitting the straight on the next card, the implied odds are significant given the stack sizes; if I hit my straight, I can likely extract more value from Elon, who is showing strength on a board that likely connects with his big blind calling range. I will call to see the turn.

On the turn, the board has become significantly more dangerous with the arrival of the Queen of Diamonds, which completes a potential flush. My hand, King of Hearts and Seven of Spades, still holds an open-ended straight draw, but I do not have a diamond in my hand to act as a blocker or backup. Given that I am out of position against Elon, who showed strength on the flop, checking is the best course of action. This allows me to gauge Elon's reaction to the new board texture and potentially realize my equity if the price to call a bet is right, or take a free card if he checks behind.

Heads-up on turn after Minni folded preflop raiser range. Board 8s9d6dQd is draw-heavy: diamond flush possible (three diamonds), straights like T7 completed, Q improved top pair. Hold bottom pair (6s) with nut flush blocker (Ah, though hearts) and strong Ace kicker, good vs Flash's wide SB defend-call range (worse pairs like 77/Tx/7x, missed diamond/straight draws, floats/A-high). Flop check-call my donk suggests medium strength not super strong (no sets likely cold-called pre). Turn check weakness. Effective SPR ~2.3 (Flash 4900). Bet 2/3 pot (1400) for thin value from worse (smaller pairs, Ace-high), protection vs gutshots (5-7, J10)/draw chasers, deny free river. Checking risks giving free card to equity or induces check-behind/check-raise. Continue flop line aggression balances value/protection; low risk as villain commits ~30% stack. Maximizes EV by charging weak range while controlling pot size.

On the turn, the Queen of Diamonds has arrived, completing potential flushes. Elon has led both the flop and the turn with significant sizing. My open-ended straight draw (8 outs) gives me roughly 17% equity to improve on the river, but the pot odds of 1,400 to win 4,900 are only 28.5%. Because the board is highly connected and flush-heavy, my straight might not even be the best hand if it hits (especially if the 5d or Td completes it), and Elon's betting pattern suggests a strong made hand. With roughly 49 BBs remaining, I will fold to preserve my stack for a better opportunity.

I am on the button with Ace-Four offsuit. In a three-handed game, any Ace on the button is a standard opening hand. My stack is relatively short at 49 BBs (4900 chips), while my opponents Elon and Minni have much larger stacks. Raising to 300 is a standard open here to put pressure on the blinds and potentially take the pot down pre-flop or play a pot with position. If I am re-raised, I will have to evaluate based on the size, but folding is not an option for the initial action.

Jack-five offsuit is a trash hand with poor playability and low equity against a button raiser's range. Flash is aggressive and often raises wide from the button to steal blinds. I'm out of position in the small blind against two opponents (Minni still to act), and calling 250 into a 450 pot offers poor implied odds for such a weak holding. Folding preserves my deep stack (15.8k) for better spots while avoiding dominated postflop situations.

I'm in the big blind with T♥3♠ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450). Calling costs me 200 more, bringing my total committed to 300 and giving me pot odds of ~31% (200/650). Flash on the button has been opening frequently to 3bb so his range should be relatively wide — T3o has marginal but reasonable equity vs a wide range, and the call is small relative to my deep stack. I will defend the blind to realize equity and play the flop cautiously out of position: continue if I pair a ten/three or pick up strong draws, fold to heavy sustained aggression on dry broadway/overcards. For these reasons I will call the 200 (total commit 300).

I missed this flop — holding T♥3♠ on A♥4♥K♦ gives me no pair and only a very weak backdoor heart possibility (needs both turn and river). I’m out of position vs the button’s raising range and betting as a bluff here is thin and likely to be called or raised. I’ll check to control the pot and fold to meaningful continuation aggression; if the button checks back I get a free card to improve.

I have flopped two pair (Aces and Fours) on a board of Ah-4h-Kd. This is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. Minni checked to me as the pre-flop aggressor. There is a flush draw and some broadway straight possibilities (QJ) on the board, so I need to bet for value and protection. I will bet 400 into the 650 pot, which is a standard continuation bet size that targets weaker Aces, Kings, and heart draws.

I have T♥3♠ on A♥4♥K♦ — no pair, only a backdoor heart and 6 weak outs (three tens, three threes). Flash’s bet is 400 into the pre-bet pot (pot now 1050), so calling 400 requires ~38% equity (400/1050) to break even. My realistic equity to improve by the river (~24% to pair by river, ~4% backdoor flush) is well below that and much of my equity is dominated by Ax/Kx and broadway hands in a button c-betting range. I’m out of position, the bet size is standard and I don’t want to invest more with such a marginal hand — preserve the deep stack and fold.

Aces-Ten suited is a premium hand on the button with excellent playability, position, and equity against calling ranges. The pot is 150 with only 100 to call, likely due to a small blind limp or complete from Minni and a check from Flash in the big blind. With deep stacks relative to the blinds (my 158BB, Minni 189BB, Flash 51BB), this is a standard spot to raise for value and isolation. A 3x raise to 300 pressures the limper and builds the pot while denying equity to weaker hands. Folding or limping would be too passive, and over-raising risks unnecessary ICM pressure early in the tournament phase (hand 96/150). History shows Minni and Flash call raises but fold weak hands postflop often.

I’m in the small blind with 8♦5♦ facing a standard button open to 300. The pot is 450 and it costs me 250 to call, so I need roughly 36% equity to justify a cold call — 8‑5 suited is well below that vs a BTN opening range and I’ll be out of position post‑flop (and possibly multiway if the short-stacked BB calls). A small 3‑bet bluff is possible in some spots, but with deep stacks and an unknown response from the short BB it’s an unnecessary risk with a very marginal hand. Optimal, low-variance play here is to fold and wait for a better spot.

I am in the big blind with 9c6s. The button, Elon, has raised to 300. To call, I need to put in 200 more chips into a pot that will be 550. This gives me odds of 2.75:1. However, my hand is relatively weak and out of position against a player with a large stack. Given my current stack of 5150, I should be selective about the pots I enter out of position. I've observed Elon being quite active and aggressive recently. I will fold this hand and look for a better spot to defend my blind or take a stand with a stronger starting hand.

I’m on the button with Q♠3♠ — a marginal but playable suited queen with position and a queen-blocker. There’s only the blinds to act, pot is 150 and it costs 100 to play. Against a short SB (Flash 5100) and a deeper BB (Elon 15850) I want to apply pressure and try to steal the blinds or isolate heads-up in position. A standard 2–3x open is appropriate here: raise to 300 (3×BB) keeps the pot manageable, preserves fold equity, and avoids bloating the pot unnecessarily with a speculative hand. Plan: if both fold I pick up the blinds; if SB shoves I’ll evaluate/call given my stack and reasonable equity vs a wide shove; if Elon 3-bets big I’ll usually fold — don’t play OOP with Q3s vs a large 3-bet. Postflop I’ll play aggressively in position on favourable boards and exercise pot control or check back when multiway.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Nine suited, a strong hand in three-handed play. The Button (Minni) has been raising frequently, and K9s is well ahead of a typical wide button-opening range. While calling is an option, three-betting allows me to take the initiative and potentially win the pot pre-flop, or at least isolate the Button. Given my stack size of 5,100 chips (51 BB), a three-bet to 1,000 is a standard sizing that puts pressure on the opener while leaving room to maneuver post-flop if called.

5s2c is an extremely weak offsuit hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. Facing a button open-raise to 300 from Minni followed by a 3-bet to 1000 from Flash in the small blind, who has a small stack of 4150 and is showing aggression. My hand has negligible equity against their likely strong ranges (Flash's 3-bet range is polarized towards premium hands or bluffs, but 52o crushes poorly to both). Pot odds to call 900 into 1400 are insufficient for the dominated equity and terrible postflop playability. Folding preserves my deep stack of 15850 for better spots in this tournament with 150 hands total.

Call to 1000 (I put a total of 1,000 in the pot). Q♠3♠ is marginal but suited and I have the button — position is a big advantage. Flash’s SB 3‑bet to 1,000 (leaving ~3,150 behind) is aggressive but not huge, so his range can be fairly wide; calling gives me ~33% pot odds to see a flop and lets me realize my equity and use position. 4‑betting as a bluff is thin with Q3s (poor blockers and dominated vs likely value hands), and folding surrenders too much equity. Postflop plan: proceed aggressively on favourable flops (spade draws, top pair/backdoor combos), control the pot on dry misses and fold to large, polarized aggression — reevaluate if Flash jams.

As the pre-flop 3-better, this Ace-high, dry board (Ac-4h-7d) significantly favors my perceived range. I am expected to have all the strong Aces (AK, AQ, AJ) that Minni might have folded or only partially called with. Although K9s has missed this flop, it's a prime candidate for a continuation bet to exploit my range advantage and force folds from her non-Ace hands like pocket pairs (88-JJ) or suited connectors. A bet of around 30% of the pot (650) is effective on such a static board to maintain initiative without over-committing my remaining stack.

I have Q♠3♠ on A♣4♥7♦ — I completely missed the flop. Flash 3‑bet preflop and now makes a small c‑bet (650 into a ~2100 pot), which is a sizing consistent with a wide continuing range (AX, broadways, and bluffs). Calling 650 is cheap (you need ~19% equity to justify the call: 650 into a 2750 pot -> ~19% break‑even) and keeps the pot manageable while giving me position to float and take the pot away on the turn if he shows weakness. Raising as a bluff with Q3s is thin — I have poor blockers and can be crushed by any Ace or pair. Plan: call to see the turn; if the turn brings a spade/Q/3 or villain checks I can apply pressure, but if he shows large, polarized aggression or jams I fold (he’s short‑stacked and his shoves will be heavily weighted toward value). Calling preserves maximum post‑flop options and exploits his likely wide flop bet.

The turn 8h is a blank for my hand (Ks9s) and potentially improves some of Minni's calling range (like 78s or A8s). After she called the pre-flop 3-bet and the flop continuation bet on an Ace-high board, her range is heavily weighted toward Aces or medium pocket pairs. Since my stack is now nearly equal to the size of the pot, a second barrel is extremely risky; if she has an Ace, she is unlikely to fold, and I would be effectively pot-committed. Checking allows me to see if she checks behind with a marginal hand or to fold if she bets, preserving my remaining 35 big blinds for a better spot.

Board is A♣ 4♥ 7♦ 8♥, I hold Q♠3♠ and Flash checked the turn after c-betting the flop. I completely missed — I have only 6 outs to pair on the river (~13%) and no realistic backdoor flush/straight. Flash’s line (SB 3‑bet pre, flop c‑bet then check) is consistent with many Ax and some medium pairs/air; a bluff here could work but would require a fairly large sizing to fold his better holdings and I don’t want to commit a big portion of my stack with very little raw equity. Pot control and position are my friends: check the turn, see a cheap river, and then re-evaluate (bet for value if I pair/river is favorable, bluff small only if he gives a clear checkback, fold to large raises). Checking avoids gambling away chips with a marginal bluff and preserves my stack edge for later spots.

The board has paired on the river with an Eight, making the final board Ac-4h-7d-8h-8d. My King-high has no showdown value. However, Minni called a 3-bet pre-flop and a continuation bet on an Ace-high flop, which strongly suggests she has an Ace or a pocket pair. After we both checked the turn, her range is likely capped at a weak Ace or a medium pair. Attempting a bluff here would require committing most of my remaining stack, and given Minni's chip lead and previous calling tendencies, she is unlikely to fold any pair. Checking is the most prudent play to see if I can get to a cheap showdown or fold to a bet, preserving my stack for better opportunities.

I have Q♠3♠ on A♣ 4♥ 7♦ 8♥ 8♦ — essentially no showdown value (queen‑high). Flash 3‑bet pre to 1,000, c‑bet the flop 650 (I called), then checked turn and checked river. Pot is 3,400 and Flash is short (~3,500).

Why check: Betting has almost no value — I can’t credibly represent trips or Ax (I don’t block A or 8) and I have no pair to extract value. Bluffing is thin: opponent’s line (preflop 3‑bet + flop c‑bet then checks turns) contains a lot of Ax, medium pairs and some air; many of those hands will call a river bet, and with Flash’s short stack a bet could induce a shove that I can’t profitably call with Q‑high. A small bet might fold the very weakest part of his range but will also cost chips and risk a shove/call by better hands; overall negative EV.

Checking preserves my stack, gives me the cheapest showdown, and avoids getting exploited by a shove or a call from A/7/8x. If Flash now bets I’ll re‑evaluate on sizing (fold to large jams; consider a fold/call depending on how large and how often he bluffs). Solid pot control + fold avoidance is the right play here.

I am on the button with 7h3d. This is a very weak hand in a three-handed game. Although the pot is small and it only costs 100 to call, the hand has almost no playability post-flop and I am out of position against the small blind if I call and the big blind checks. Folding is the most disciplined and standard play here to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

In the small blind with T7o after button folds. This hand has poor playability postflop out of position, limited straight potential offsuit, and low equity against BB defending range. Minni is aggressive and deep-stacked; folding avoids speculative OOP spots and preserves stack for better opportunities in late tournament with 3 players left.

Pocket Aces (Ad Ah) is the best possible starting hand in Hold'em. I'm on the button with excellent position on both opponents: Minni in SB (deep stack 17250) and Flash in BB (shallower 6800). The pot is 150 preflop with a to-call of 100, indicating a likely limp or check from blinds. Raising is mandatory to build the pot, get value from worse hands (dominated Aces, broadways, pairs), and thin the field to maximize AA's equity heads-up or 3-way. A raise to 900 (9x BB, ~2.5x pot + to-call) provides good fold equity against trash while committing appropriately relative to effective stacks (Flash's 68BB). Smaller raises invite multiway pots where AA plays poorly; larger risks overplaying but stacks allow it. From hand history, opponents call raises frequently but fold to aggression postflop often. This sizes up the pot nicely for value.

Fold. 7♦4♠ is a very weak, unplayable hand — especially out of position from the SB. Elon opened to 900 (≈9x BB); I must call 850 to continue. The pot is 1050, so calling 850 would make the pot 1900 and require ~44.7% equity to break even heads-up (850/1900) — 7‑4o has far less vs an opener. There's also the risk of a multiway pot if the BB defends. With a healthy stack (17,250) and plenty of hands left, preserving chips and waiting for a better spot is correct. No reason to 3-bet or defend here.

Elon has opened with an unusually large raise of 9x the big blind (900 chips) from the button. While A5o is a hand I would normally defend in the big blind against a standard 2x or 3x raise in a 3-handed game, the 9x sizing significantly changes the pot odds and the risk-to-reward ratio. Defending out of position against such a large sizing with a hand that is easily dominated and has poor playability is likely negative EV. Given my stack of 68bb, I am the short stack and prefer to wait for a better spot rather than committing nearly 12% of my stack to see a flop with a marginal Ace-high hand.

I’m on the button with A♥Q♣ — a strong, standard open-raise hand. Only the blinds are to act, so I want to seize the initiative, isolate, and get value from worse aces/broadways while avoiding playing a bloated multiway pot out of position. Table norm is ~3x the BB; I’ll raise to 300 (total) — big enough to discourage limp/calls from the small blind but small enough to keep stacks deep for post‑flop play. Plan: c‑bet favorable textures and re-evaluate if faced with a sizable 3‑bet (will proceed cautiously vs large 3‑bet depending on opponent and sizing).

I am holding 8h 7c in the small blind. The button (Minni) has raised to 300. 87 offsuit is a weak hand to play out of position, especially with a stack of 67.5 big blinds while facing the chip leader who has been very active. Calling puts me in a difficult spot post-flop without the initiative, and 3-betting with this hand is too risky given the current stack distributions. The best course of action to preserve my tournament life is to fold and wait for a stronger opening.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot is 450, calling 200 more gives pot odds of about 3.25:1 (pot will be 650), requiring roughly 23% equity. My hand 6h4c is offsuit, disconnected, with very low playability OOP. Against a typical wide button opening range, it has poor equity (~30-35% at best) and even worse postflop potential: few strong draws, dominated often, hard to realize equity. Minni is aggressive from history but stacks are deep; folding trash preserves stack for better spots. No reason to defend this wide.

I have been dealt pocket aces, the strongest starting hand in poker. Being on the button, I want to build a pot and extract value. My standard open raise size throughout this tournament has been 300, so I will stick with that to remain balanced and avoid telegraphing the strength of my hand while still building the pot against Elon and Minni.

Preflop in the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (2.5BB) with Th6d offsuit. This is a speculative offsuit hand with poor connectivity, dominated equity against typical button raising ranges, and terrible playability out of position postflop. My stack is 158xBB effective, but folding marginal hands OOP preserves stack for better spots. Button raisers like Flash open wide, but calling here leads to difficult decisions on most boards. Pot odds aren't compelling enough (calling 250 into 450 is ~36% equity needed, but hand realizes <30% vs wide range). Standard fold to maximize long-term EV.

7♣2♥ is the worst possible starting hand — disconnected, offsuit, and heavily dominated by any reasonable button opening range. Flash opened to 300; I’ve already posted the 100 BB, and calling 200 to see a flop would be investing 200 into a 450 pot (pot after call = 650), so I need roughly 30.8% equity to break even. 7-2 offsuit has far less equity vs a button's opening range and I'd be out of position postflop, so calling is a losing long-term play. A 3-bet bluff is possible in some spots, but 7-2o has terrible blockers and risk/reward here is poor given Flash's stack (≈64 BB) and my deeper stack — no need to bloat the pot with a junk hand. Fold and wait for a better spot.

On the button in a 3-handed pot after the SB folds and BB checks, this is an excellent stealing opportunity. 2h7h is a suited connector with potential to flop strong draws like flush or straight draws, making it playable multiway if called. Raising applies fold equity against the blinds, who have shown loose tendencies in prior hands but will fold weak holdings here often. A standard 3x BB raise to 300 builds the pot when ahead or takes it down immediately. Deep stacks allow for postflop flexibility if needed. Folding is too tight, calling is passive; raising maximizes EV long-term.

I'm in the small blind with K♥6♥ facing a button open to 300 (3bb). This is a defendable holding vs a wide opener in 3-handed play — suited King has decent equity and good backdoor heart/straight potential. Calling 250 more (300 total in the pot) gives me ~36% break-even odds to see a flop and keeps the pot manageable; a tiny 3-bet to 500 would have limited fold equity, likely invite a squeeze from the BB (Flash, 6800) or a call from the opener, and commits me OOP for multi-street play. With deep stacks and many hands left in the tournament I prefer lower-variance play: call and evaluate postflop (fold to big preflop 3-bets, c-bet/raise selectively if I connect or have strong draws, otherwise control the pot).

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Ten of diamonds, a strong premium hand in a 3-handed game. Elon raised from the button, which is standard, and Minni called from the small blind. The pot currently has 700 chips and it costs 200 more for me to call. While calling is an option to see a flop with good pot odds, 3-betting (raising) is the superior play here. It allows me to take control of the hand, potentially win the pot pre-flop, and extract value from weaker aces or broadway hands that Elon or Minni might be playing. Given my stack size of 6800 and the pot size, a raise to 1000 is a standard size that puts pressure on both opponents while maintaining a healthy stack if they continue.

Facing a 3-bet from Flash in the BB after my button open and Minni's call, 2h7h has very poor equity against a strong 3-bet range (AA-JJ, AK, AQ, etc.). Suited connectors like this play well multiway when cheap or stealing blinds, but here the pot is 1600 and calling 700 OOP in a likely multiway pot is -EV due to reverse implied odds and domination risks. Flash's stack of 5900 suggests commitment if they have it, and Minni's call doesn't strengthen my hand. Folding preserves my deep 15.5k stack for superior spots in this 3-handed dynamic where I can steal effectively with better holdings. Long-term stack maximization requires discipline here.

I opened the pot with Kh6h from the small blind vs Elon’s 300 and called to see a flop, but Flash (BB) just 3‑bet to 1000. I’ve already put 300 in and would need to call 700 more; the pot is 1600 so a call requires roughly 23–24% equity (≈3.3:1). Against a BB 3‑bet range heads‑up in 3‑handed play that range is weighted to A‑x, broadways and pocket pairs that frequently dominate K6s, and I’ll be out of position multi‑street with a marginal hand. A small 4‑bet won’t fold many better hands and a shove is unnecessary high variance when I have plenty of chips to wait for a better spot. Given the poor equity, positional disadvantage, and tournament situation I fold and preserve my stack.

I have QQ on the button with two players to act (SB Flash and BB Elon). Stacks are deep (my stack ~170bb), so this is a premium hand to build the pot and isolate. I will open-raise to 3x the BB (300 total) — large enough to charge the blinds and get information but small enough to keep weaker hands and postflop playability. Plan: if called, continuation-bet good flops to extract value; if faced with a small 3-bet I’ll consider a 4-bet to isolate or a call depending on sizing and tendencies; if faced with a very large 3-bet I’ll re-evaluate (likely call or fold to extreme pressure depending on sizing). Overall objective: extract value and use position.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Six offsuit. Minni has opened from the button for 3x the big blind. While A6o is a marginal hand to play out of position, it contains an Ace blocker, making it a viable candidate for a 3-bet semi-bluff. I have successfully used a 1000-chip 3-bet recently to take down pots pre-flop (Hands 91, 102). By raising here, I aim to win the pot immediately or narrow the field, avoiding a multi-way pot against the big stacks of Minni and Elon.

Facing a button raise to 300 from Minni, who is frequently aggressive in late position based on hand history, and a 3-bet to 1000 from Flash in the small blind with a 65BB stack. My hand is A♦3♣ offsuit, which has decent blocker value but poor playability, weak kicker, and little speculative potential against two raisers likely holding stronger ranges. Pot odds are about 1.55:1 for a call of 900 into 1400, but implied odds are poor given the strength required postflop and risk of multiway or further action. Folding preserves my deep 154BB stack for better spots in this late tournament stage with 47 hands remaining, avoiding variance with a marginal hand.

Flash (SB) 3-bet to 1000 after my 300 button open. Effective stacks are ~65bb (Flash 6500), and Flash’s small 3-bet sizing suggests a fairly wide/aggressive range (broadways, mid pairs, bluffs). QQ is a premium — ahead of most of his range and only really dominated by AA/KK — so 4-betting for value and isolation is the highest EV play. I want a size that extracts value and gives fold equity without committing the stacks; I’ll 4-bet to 3500 total (≈3.5x his 3-bet). If he calls I’ll play a big pot in position and c-bet favorable textures; if he shows extreme strength (large 5-bet shove) I’ll re-evaluate vs his sizing and range, but this sizing puts pressure on his wide hands and gets value from worse.

I am facing a 4-bet from Minni after my 3-bet semi-bluff with Ace-Six offsuit. While I have an Ace blocker, Minni's 4-bet sizing to 3500 is very strong and indicates a premium range (likely JJ+, AK) that dominates my hand. If I call the additional 2500, I would be left with only 3000 chips in a 7100 pot, effectively committing myself to a pot where I am likely a significant underdog. Shoving is also not viable as Minni is unlikely to fold better hands given the pot odds. Folding now preserves my remaining 5500 chips (55 big blinds), allowing me to wait for a better spot to recoup my losses.

I am on the button with K3o. In a three-handed game, King-high on the button is a reasonable hand to enter the pot, but my stack is relatively short (6500 chips, 65 big blinds). Looking at the table dynamics, Minni (Big Blind) and Elon (Small Blind) have been quite aggressive. I've folded to several of their raises and 3-bets lately. However, K3o is on the weaker side of my button opening range. Given the aggressiveness of the blinds, I will fold to avoid getting involved in a potentially expensive pot with a marginal hand out of position post-flop if they defend or 3-bet.

Preflop in SB with 5d9d suited connectors. Flash folded, heads-up vs Minni in BB. Pot 150, already posted SB 50, need 50 more to call for pot odds of over 3:1. This hand has good implied odds and playability postflop due to suited straight potential. Folding too tight from SB; raising not strong enough for value or bluff here with Minni's aggressive history. Standard complete/call.

Heads-up vs Elon who merely limped the small blind. Qc8h is a hand I want to defend the big blind with but it’s not strong enough to force the issue preflop out of position with deep stacks (~180bb). There’s no cost to checking and seeing a flop, which keeps the pot small and preserves stack depth for better post-flop decisions. If the flop gives me a pair, good draw, or a favourable board texture I’ll proceed aggressively; otherwise I’ll use pot control and fold to large pressure OOP. Prefer to check here rather than thin‑value or iso‑raise with a marginal holding.

Heads-up out of position on a Qd Ah 4d flop with 5d9d, giving me the nut diamond flush draw (9 outs, ~35% equity) plus two overcards and backdoor straight potential. Pot is 200, acting first. This board is dry-ish but scary with A-high and flush draw possibility, which favors my range as the limper/SB completer. Minni checked preflop after my limp, suggesting a wide BB defending range. Betting semi-bluffs for fold equity, protection, and to build pot with my strong equity while denying her equity. Half-pot sizing (125) balances value from worse Ax/draws and bluff-catching hands while keeping pot manageable OOP if she raises. Checking risks giving free cards to her draws or allowing her to rep the A. Long-term stack maximization favors aggressive play with premium draws in positionally disadvantaged spots against Minni's history of calling stations in pots.

I have top pair (Qc) with a weak 8 kicker on Qd Ah 4d. Heads-up vs Elon (limp SB then C-bet 125 into a 325 pot) his range is wide — Ax, Qx, small pairs, and some diamond draws. The bet is small; calling 125 requires ~28% equity (125 / (325+125) ≈ 27.8%), which my hand should have against a realistic c-bet/float range. Raising to the 250 min-raise would bloat the pot and polarize me to better hands (most Aces, sets) while folding out worse hands I can extract value from later. With deep stacks I'd rather control the pot now and see a turn. Plan: call the flop, re-evaluate on the turn — proceed for value if I improve or the turn is safe, fold to large aggression if an Ace/diamond pairs the board and he shows strength, or apply modest pressure on a blank.

On the turn with nut diamond flush draw (9d5d on Qd Ah 4d Kh board), I have ~20% equity to hit the nuts by river, plus backdoor straight/overcard potential. OOP vs Minni after she called my flop c-bet, her range includes Ax, pocket pairs, weaker draws, and some made hands like sets or two-pair. The Kh turn improves some Ax to two-pair (AK) and brings broadway straight draws (e.g., JT), making the board scarier, but denies some straight draws and doesn't complete hearts. Pot 450, acting first, deep stacks (15k effective). Checking risks giving free cards to her equity or facing a bet into a bigger pot OOP; betting semi-bluffs for fold equity against marginal hands/draws, gets value from worse Ax/pairs/draws, protects against her improving, and builds pot for when I hit. Half-pot sizing (225) balances thin value, bluffs well, keeps SPR manageable if called (SPR ~30 post-call), and continues my flop aggression to rep strength. Minni's history shows she calls bets but folds to continued pressure often; this maximizes EV long-term.

I have Qc8h on Qd Ah 4d Kh — top pair with a weak kicker. Elon limped the SB and has been betting small (wide range: Ax, Qx, Kx, small pairs, some diamond combos and bluffs). He’s bet 225 into the ~450 pot (pot is 675 now), so calling 225 requires ~25% equity; my hand has enough showdown value heads‑up to justify a call.
